name: PyCaret

performance.notes: Low-code wrapper; performance determined by underlying sklearn/XGBoost models

agent_readiness.notes: Low-code AutoML library wrapping scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost. Modules: classification, regression, clustering, anomaly detection, time series, NLP. compare_models() benchmarks 20+ algorithms automatically. MLflow/W&B integration. deploy_model() to cloud. Best for data analysts and non-ML-engineers who want production-quality models with minimal code. Extremely fast prototyping.

gotchas: Magic functions hide complexity — hard to debug underlying issues
Less control than direct scikit-learn for custom pipelines
Some modules (NLP, time series) less mature than specialized libraries
best_when: Data analysts needing ML models without deep ML expertise
Rapid prototyping and model comparison across many algorithms
Jupyter notebook workflows for exploratory ML
Business analysts generating model reports for stakeholders
avoid_when: Production ML engineering requiring fine control (use sklearn directly)
Deep learning workflows (wraps classical ML only)
When interpretability and custom pipelines are critical
summary: PyCaret is a low-code AutoML library that wraps scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost with a simple 3-function API: setup(), compare_models(), predict_model(). Automatically benchmarks 20+ algorithms. Modules for classification, regression, clustering, anomaly detection, and time series. Best for data analysts wanting production-quality models with minimal ML expertise. Fast prototyping in Jupyter notebooks.
